page: Microsoft Azure D32ps_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine instance belonging to the DPSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores powered by an Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor operating at 3.0 GHz. The CPU architecture is arm64, with one thread per core and no hyper-threading. Cache specifications include 64 KB of L1 instruction/data cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark evaluations place the D32ps_v5 in the average performance tier across most compute and memory operations. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance score is 57217.14, while the single-core score is 1822.98. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 2231.53 GB/sec, dropping to 170.09 GB/sec for large block uncached reads, and 77.1 GB/sec for large block writes. In application-specific benchmarks, the server achieves 41.25 million operations per second in Redis SET operations and a static web serving throughput of 32.88 GB/sec. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 1104.0. Large-scale language model inference is a notable bottleneck, with prompt processing for a 70B model yielding a poor result of 3.58 tokens per second.

The D32ps_v5 offers qualitative cost efficiency by leveraging the ARM64 architecture, which typically delivers a high physical core density and lower power consumption compared to traditional x86 architectures. The dedicated core allocation ensures consistent compute performance without resource contention. However, the lack of local storage requires external disk attachment, and the absence of AVX or AMX instruction sets limits its utility for advanced vector mathematics or heavy machine learning workloads. The instance is suited for general-purpose workloads, multi-threaded applications, software compilation, database operations, and static web serving.
description: Microsoft Azure D32ps_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine instance from the DPSv5 family, featuring 32 dedicated physical cores on an Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor running at 3.0 GHz. It is configured with 128.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio, and operates on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Benchmarks indicate average multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 57217.14 and a static web serving throughput of 32.88 GB/sec, though single-core performance and large LLM inference are weak. This server is suited for general-purpose workloads, multi-threaded software compilation, database operations, and web serving, offering qualitative cost efficiency through its high-density ARM architecture.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32ps_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated Ampere Altra cores, 128 GB of RAM, and average multi-core benchmark performance, suited for general-purpose workloads and web serving.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32ps_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine with 32 dedicated Ampere Altra cores and 128 GB RAM, suited for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: An ARM-powered virtual machine offering thirty-two dedicated cores and balanced memory allocation for consistent multi-threaded general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 32-core Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor at 3.0 GHz
Equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM providing 4.0 GB of memory per core
Delivers average multi-core performance with 41.25 million Redis SET operations per second
Lacks local storage and GPU hardware but supports Premium Storage options
Suited for general-purpose applications, web serving, and software compilation
